_comment: Two ways to feed the provisioner. (A) Flat fields, fully resolved, as below: used as-is. (B) Raw `answers` keyed by question id (see the example block): the host maps them to these flat fields, deterministically by default or with --enrich. You can also include both; flat fields win where present.
team_structure: owner plus two part-time field techs, no office staff
top_office_work: scheduling routes, writing up quotes for new yards, chasing late payments
workloads: route quoting, recurring invoicing, follow-up on overdue accounts
tools: Gmail, a Google Sheet for the customer list, no real invoicing tool yet
ideal_customer: multi-dog homes and small HOAs on recurring weekly plans
friction_customer: one-time deep-clean tire-kickers who price-shop and never rebook

answers.q1_business: Scoop Dogg, dog waste pickup for yards, been at it about 3 years
answers.q2_team: it's me plus two part-time guys who do the routes, no office help
answers.q3_office_work: scheduling routes, quoting new yards, and chasing people who pay late
answers.q4_tools: just Gmail and a Google Sheet for customers, no invoicing software
answers.q5_money: roughly 120k a year, average is a single yard weekly at about 25 bucks
answers.q6_ideal_customer: multi-dog houses and small HOAs that want weekly recurring service
answers.q7_friction_customer: one-time deep cleans who shop around on price and never come back
